message:
bubbles: implement Go-parity duration formatting for timer

The format_duration function now matches Go's time.Duration.String() behavior
exactly, same as the stopwatch implementation:
- Sub-second durations use ms, µs, or ns units (e.g., "100ms", "500µs")
- Seconds with sub-second precision show decimals (e.g., "5.05s", "5.001s")
- Minutes and seconds format as "1m30s", hours as "1h0m0s"
- Trailing zeros are trimmed from decimal parts

Added comprehensive Go parity tests covering:
- Sub-second durations (ms, µs, ns)
- Seconds with decimal precision
- Minutes with and without sub-second precision
- Hours with and without sub-second precision
- Timer countdown progression verification
- Zero duration edge case

This addresses bead bd-212m.6.2 (timer parity audit).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
